# Contract: `intent.json` schema delta — disburse rationale references

**Phase**: 1 (design & contracts)
**Surface**: `lib/Amaru/Treasury/IntentJSON/Schema.hs` (JSON Schema for `intent.json`)
**Schema file artefact**: `assets/schema/intent.schema.json` (regenerated by `just update-schema`)

## Backward compatibility

The new `references` field is **optional** on the disburse rationale
block. Every existing `intent.json` file (every checked-in fixture +
any operator-saved intent) continues to validate unchanged.

| Pre-change intent | Post-change intent | Validates against new schema? |
|---|---|---|
| No `rationale.references` field | No `rationale.references` field | yes — defaults to `[]` |
| No `rationale.references` field | `rationale.references: []` | yes |
| N/A | `rationale.references: [{uri, label}]` | yes — `@type` defaults to `"Other"` |
| N/A | `rationale.references: [{uri, @type, label}]` | yes |

## Schema entry

Added inside the disburse rationale block schema in
`Amaru.Treasury.IntentJSON.Schema`. The block currently has fields
`event`, `label`, `description`, `destinationLabel`, `justification`.
This change adds `references` as a sibling, optional, with a default
of `[]`.

```json
{
  "references": {
    "type": "array",
    "default": [],
    "items": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["uri", "label"],
      "properties": {
        "uri": {
          "type": "string",
          "minLength": 1,
          "maxLength": 256,
          "description": "Full URI of the reference document (e.g. ipfs://<CID>, https://..., ar://...). Chunked per the metadatum 64-byte cap at serialisation time."
        },
        "@type": {
          "type": "string",
          "minLength": 1,
          "maxLength": 64,
          "default": "Other",
          "description": "SundaeSwap reference type. Default 'Other'."
        },
        "label": {
          "type": "string",
          "minLength": 1,
          "maxLength": 256,
          "description": "Human-readable label. Literal ' - ' marks the split for the on-chain Metadatum 3-chunk shape."
        }
      },
      "additionalProperties": false
    }
  }
}
```

## Wire-shape mapping

Aeson FromJSON/ToJSON instances for `RationaleJSON` (the JSON-side
projection of `RationaleBody`) and `RationaleReferenceJSON` (mirror
of `RationaleReference`). The wire shape uses `@type` with the
literal at-sign; aeson's `.:` and `.=` handle this fine.

```haskell
instance FromJSON RationaleReferenceJSON where
    parseJSON = withObject "RationaleReferenceJSON" $ \o ->
        RationaleReferenceJSON
            <$> o .:  "uri"
            <*> o .:? "@type" .!= "Other"
            <*> o .:  "label"

instance ToJSON RationaleReferenceJSON where
    toJSON RationaleReferenceJSON{..} =
        object
            [ "uri"   .= rjrUri
            , "@type" .= rjrType
            , "label" .= rjrLabel
            ]
```

When `rjReferences` is empty, `ToJSON RationaleJSON` should still
emit `"references": []` (not omit the field) so the schema and the
emitted JSON are symmetric. The schema's `default: []` covers the
read path even when the field is absent.

## Schema regeneration

The repo's `just update-schema` recipe writes
`assets/schema/intent.schema.json` from the Haskell schema module.
After the S2 slice (`Amaru.Treasury.IntentJSON.Schema` change), the
worker pair runs:

```bash
nix develop --quiet -c just update-schema
nix develop --quiet -c just schema-check
```

Both must pass. The diff to `assets/schema/intent.schema.json` ships
in the same commit (S2).

## Round-trip property

A QuickCheck property in the test suite (added in S2):

> For every well-formed `RationaleReferenceJSON`,
> `decode (encode r) == Just r`.

Existing round-trip properties for `RationaleJSON` continue to hold.

## Out of schema

- `destination.details.anchorUrl` / `destination.details.anchorDataHash`
  — separate ticket if/when needed.
- Per-reference `anchorDataHash` — separate ticket.
- A general-purpose URL chunker — not in schema; the schema accepts
  URIs up to 256 bytes and serialisation fails loudly past the
  IPFS-aware 64-byte chunk cap.
